metrics.constraint_note: measured against the PARSED profile; natural-language -> structured parsing errors are a separate, upstream concern not captured here
metrics.adversarial_note: self-authored set: 10 trials, one injected per brief, ROTATED across 5 attack categories (carat/number in opinion, absent certification, wrong metal in opinion, wrong stone in opinion, currency in opinion). Demonstrates coverage of these KNOWN patterns only — NOT robustness to all phrasings, and NOT to factual terms outside the controlled vocabulary (those are not scanned).

subjective_relevance.judge_prompt: You are judging how relevant a jewellery recommendation is to a shopper's request, on a 1-5 scale (5 = perfect fit). Consider style, occasion, metal/stone preference and budget. Return a score and whether it is relevant.
subjective_relevance.caveat: LLM-as-judge estimate over a small self-built brief set. This is NOT validated relevance accuracy: there is no human ground truth and the judge is itself an LLM. Treat as directional only.

report:
# Eval report — eval-b6da8e681333
Dataset: generated:eval_briefs(n=10)  |  Provider: anthropic  |  Embedder: hashing-tf-512  |  Cost: $0.2918

## Structural guarantees (provider-independent — deterministic code, NOT accuracy)
- Factual claims grounded in output: 93.0% (N=172 claims) — by construction (facts rendered from the record)
- Hard-constraint satisfaction: 100.0% (N=29 items) — vs the PARSED profile (measured against the PARSED profile; natural-language -> structured parsing errors are a separate, upstream concern not captured here)
- Retrieval validity: 100.0% (N=29)
- Adversarial grounding block rate: 100.0% (N=10 injected) — self-authored set: 10 trials, one injected per brief, ROTATED across 5 attack categories (carat/number in opinion, absent certification, wrong metal in opinion, wrong stone in opinion, currency in opinion). Demonstrates coverage of these KNOWN patterns only — NOT robustness to all phrasings, and NOT to factual terms outside the controlled vocabulary (those are not scanned).

## Subjective relevance (LLM-as-judge ESTIMATE — never validated accuracy)
- Mean LLM-judge score: 3.4/5 (N=10, judge=claude-sonnet-4-6)
- Caveat: LLM-as-judge estimate over a small self-built brief set. This is NOT validated relevance accuracy: there is no human ground truth and the judge is itself an LLM. Treat as directional only.

## Prompt iteration
- Variant B win rate: 90.0% (N=10, randomized order-shuffled pairwise (position bias neutralized); v1 (concise) vs v2 (occasion-enriched))
